title: Tuna pasta bake
subtitle: Tinned tuna and sweetcorn in a creamy sauce under a cheddar crust
excerpt: Tuna pasta bake is the British store-cupboard hero: tinned tuna, sweetcorn, pasta, and a white sauce topped with cheddar and baked until golden. It is fast to make, inexpensive, and consistently popular with families.
sourceNotes: Standard British store-cupboard pasta bake. Method based on the standard British home-cooking practice of combining a béchamel-style sauce with tinned fish and pasta, popularised in British households from the 1970s onward.

recipe.freezeNotes: Assemble but do not bake. Wrap and freeze for up to 2 months. Bake from frozen at 180°C for 50 minutes, foil-covered for the first 20 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles well. Use a larger tin.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble a day ahead, refrigerate, and bake as directed with an extra 5 minutes.

glossaryTerms.definition: A white sauce made by cooking flour in butter, then gradually whisking in milk until smooth and thick. One of the French mother sauces, used widely in British cooking as a base for cheese sauce and pasta bakes.

content.text: Tuna pasta bake is one of the most reliably easy things you can make from a stocked cupboard: tinned tuna and sweetcorn folded into a quick white sauce, mixed with cooked pasta, and baked under cheddar until golden. It takes about 35 minutes from start to finish.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over a medium heat. Add the flour and stir for 1 minute to cook out the raw taste. This is the beginning of a

Add the milk in a steady stream, whisking constantly, until the sauce is smooth. Continue stirring over a medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes until thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Stir in the mustard and season with salt and pepper.
Heat the oven to 190°C fan. Cook the pasta in well-salted boiling water for 2 minutes less than the packet says. Drain and transfer to a large bowl.
Fold the white sauce, tuna, and sweetcorn into the pasta. Taste and adjust the seasoning. Tip into a baking tin and smooth the top.
Cover evenly with the grated cheddar. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until the cheese is golden and bubbling. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

items.cause: The milk was added too quickly, or the flour and butter weren't fully combined before the milk went in.
items.fix: Remove from the heat and whisk vigorously. If the lumps persist, pass through a sieve.

items.cause: The pasta was overcooked before baking, absorbing all the sauce.
items.fix: Cook pasta 2 minutes less than instructed and make sure the sauce is generously coating everything before it goes in.

items.cause: The oven temperature was too low or the shelf was too low.
items.fix: Move to the top shelf. Add another handful of cheddar and bake for 5 more minutes.

items.symptom: The tuna is in large dry flakes
items.cause: It was folded in roughly, or left in large pieces.
items.fix: Break up the tuna roughly with a fork before mixing. It should be flaked throughout, not in lumps.

content.text: Tuna pasta bake is the sort of dish that nobody thinks of as a recipe, yet almost everyone in Britain knows how to make it. It belongs to the school-dinner and midweek-supper tradition: reliable, filling, and made from things that keep. The sweetcorn is optional in a strict sense but in practice it is the dish's defining characteristic.
